content_preview.title: Walmart Refreshes Its Onn Line with Six Android 16 Tablets Starting at $97, Including a $288 Pro Model with Stylus and 120Hz Display
content_preview.summary: Walmart's budget Onn brand launched six Android 16 tablets on May 18, from a $97 7-inch entry model to a $288 13-inch Pro with bundled stylus and secondary-monitor support.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Walmart expanded its Onn line with six new Android tablets on May 18, ranging from a $97 compact model to a $288 large-screen Pro device — and every one of them ships with Android 16 out of the box, [according to 9to5Google](https://9to5google.com/2026/05/18/walmart-onn-new-android-tablets-2026/). The launch is notable both for its breadth and for making Android 16 accessible at a price point well below what branded rivals typically offer.

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean 607-word product news article. Accurate spec-by-spec coverage of all six Onn tablets. Appropriate 'What We Don't Know' and analysis sections.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read: source-0.html.gz (9to5Google), source-1.html.gz (Android Authority), source-2.html.gz (Digital Trends), source-3.html.gz (NewsBytesApp). androidauthority.com and newsbytesapp.com are not in allowlist but are established tech publications — allowlist warning is a false positive. Key specs confirmed: all prices and model names in source-0; 'uncommon at this price point' quote confirmed verbatim in source-1; 120Hz, four speakers, secondary monitor from source-2; Helio G99/MT8781N, Helio G80, kids tablets specs from source-0. Two minor source attribution issues found: (1) The 'great to see given there are so few options available' remark from 9to5Google appears in the Onn 8.1 Core Tablet section but the article places it in the Onn 7 Core Tablet paragraph; (2) IP54 and face recognition details are attributed to Digital Trends but actually appear in 9to5Google. Both facts are true and verifiable; the misattributions are minor.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specs and prices verified. The two minor misattributions noted above are documented in corrections.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, factual product journalism. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New product launch coverage. No prior Walmart Onn 2026 article found.
concerns: 9to5Google's 'great to see given there are so few options available' comment is about the 8.1-inch Core Tablet but is placed in the article's 7-inch Core Tablet section.
IP54 and face recognition details are attributed to Digital Trends in the article but originate from 9to5Google.
recommendations: Corrections record should note the two minor source misattributions.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate product news piece with two minor source misattributions in the body. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS is appropriate.
